intent: In DATA_DIR there is a 10x matrix triplet for one PBMC sample — files prefixed GSM5746268_MGI0369_1_SLAB-C1-C1. Run a standard Seurat workflow in R: load, QC, normalize, PCA, neighbors, clustering, UMAP, and FindAllMarkers; show me a UMAP and the top markers per cluster.

blocks.text: Good! I can see the 10x matrix triplets. There are actually 3 samples here; I'll focus on **GSM5746268** (the first one you mentioned) as instructed. Let me read the Seurat workflow recipe to ensure I follow best practices:

blocks.content: {"status": "ok", "name": "seurat-scrna", "description": "Authoritative single-cell RNA-seq QC + clustering + cluster annotation with R/Seurat (v5).", "body": "scRNA-seq QC + clustering + annotation with R/Seurat v5 (standard PBMC3k workflow). Provision: ensure_capability('Seurat'), then library(Seurat). THE THREE CHOICES THAT DEFINE THE RESULT \u2014 surface them with present_plan before committing: (1) QC thresholds (subset cutoffs on nFeature_RNA and percent.mt); (2) number of PCs/dims (read off the ElbowPlot); (3) clustering resolution (FindClusters). Steps: ReadMtx -> CreateSeuratObject(min.cells=3,min.features=200) -> PercentageFeatureSet('^MT-') -> subset QC -> NormalizeData -> FindVariableFeatures -> ScaleData -> RunPCA -> FindNeighbors -> FindClusters -> RunUMAP -> JoinLayers -> FindAllMarkers. Load ggplot2 and dplyr explicitly; never library(tidyverse)."}

blocks.text: Perfect. Now let me present the workflow plan. I'll need to make three key decisions: **QC thresholds, number of PCs, and clustering resolution**. Since this is exploratory on a new sample, I'll use sensible defaults and show you the diagnostic plots so we can adjust if needed.

input.title: Seurat scRNA-seq QC → clustering → UMAP + markers for GSM5746268
input.summary: Load 10x triplet → QC filtering → normalization → dimension reduction → clustering → UMAP visualization and marker discovery

steps.title: Load 10x matrix triplet and create Seurat object
steps.description: Read barcodes/features/matrix from GSM5746268 prefix, create object with standard thresholds (min.cells=3, min.features=200), and compute QC metrics (nFeature_RNA, nCount_RNA, %MT)

steps.title: QC filtering
steps.description: Visualize QC metrics and filter on nFeature_RNA (200–6000 range, typical for PBMC) and percent.mt (<10% typical). We'll show the distributions first to propose thresholds.

steps.title: Normalize, find highly variable genes, scale, and run PCA
steps.description: Standard pipeline: NormalizeData → FindVariableFeatures → ScaleData → RunPCA. Show elbow plot to determine number of PCs.

steps.title: Find neighbors, cluster, and run UMAP
steps.description: FindNeighbors → FindClusters (resolution=0.8, a good exploratory default) → RunUMAP. Show UMAP colored by cluster.

steps.title: FindAllMarkers and summarize
steps.description: Identify top markers per cluster (logFC > 0.25, adjusted p < 0.05). Show a table of top 3–5 genes per cluster.

assumptions: Sample is human PBMC (based on filename context), so we'll use MT gene prefix '^MT-' (standard for human)
QC thresholds will be set conservatively (nFeature 200–6000, %MT <10%), but we'll show the distributions first so you can adjust
PCs will be chosen from the elbow plot (typically 20–40 PCs for a PBMC sample)
Clustering resolution will start at 0.8 (exploratory); you can request a variant if the resolution needs adjustment
Markers will be identified using default Seurat test (Wilcoxon) with standard cutoffs (logFC > 0.25, adj.p < 0.05)
